phrasal_verbs.meaning: give free of charge; reveal sth you are trying to hide
find sth or meet sb by chance
use as a source of money
get sth, especially sth that is hard to get
write all the necessary information on a cheque, etc
provide sth good, so that sth bad seems less important
save an amount of money for the future
save money little by little (for a specific purpose)

debt: owe sb a debt of gratitude
increase: an increase in sth (of a certain amount)

questions.sentence: My grandfather made a **wealth / fortune** by investing at the right time.
I think you'll find that the Microcar is really very **economic / economical** to drive.
I'm afraid we can only accept the return of the item if you still have the **receipt / bill**.
If you show this coupon at the **checkout / discount**, you'll receive a free gift.
I'm not keen on Justin Timberlake so I'll try to **refund / exchange** this CD for one by Blue.
The assistant called the manager when the note I gave her turned out to be **plastic / fake**.
This unbeatable special **offer / bill** is only available until the end of the week, so hurry!
Excuse me, but I don't suppose you have **make / change** for a fifty, do you?
I went back to the shop a week later, and the **price / cash** had gone up by 50%!

across.clue: Nine out of ten shoppers who tried our washing powder preferred it to any other leading

I need to find a bank because I didn't bring enough .... out with me.
Come to Barons summer .... ! Everything is down 30%!

down.clue: If you buy an extra battery at the same time as the digital camera, we can offer you a .... of 10%.
Our supermarket stocks over a thousand household .... at low, low prices!
When I checked the .... on my way out of the supermarket, I noticed that I had paid twice for the fruit.
If you take something back to the shop, you might get a .... or be offered another item instead.
Waiter! Do you think you could bring us the .... , please?
There's very little heavy industry in my country, so we import a lot of .... from abroad.

C.title: Phrasal verbs (gap fill)
C.instructions: Complete using the correct form of the phrasal verbs in the box.

questions.sentence: Of course I'll pay you back next week. You can ...... on it.
Would you please ...... out the cheque to Mr P. Edwards?
I try to ...... by a little money each year to spend on a nice holiday.
We ...... through a lot of butter in our family every week.
Do you fancy coming into town on Saturday to ...... round the shops?
The robber couldn't explain how he ...... by such a large amount of money when the police caught him.
Did you hear about the millionaire who ...... away his entire fortune to charity?

D.title: Phrasal verbs (gap fill)
D.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

questions.sentence: When I was young, we always had to do (1) ...... a lot of things that other kids had.
We lived (2) ...... my dad's wage, which wasn't much, but we managed to get (3) .......
If we needed anything, we would (4) ...... up for it, but there was never very much for luxuries.
Then, one day, I was helping my mum clean out my great-aunt's attic after she died when we came (5) ...... my great-aunt's will!
In it, she had left all her money to my mum, and my great-aunt had been very rich! We had suddenly come (6) ...... a fortune!
We bought a new house and a new car and went on a great holiday to (7) ...... up for all the times we hadn't been able to afford it.

E.title: Key word transformation (Phrases and collocations)
E.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

questions.sentence:
I suggest not spending all your money now so that you'll have some later.
**for** What about ...... later?
questions.answer: saving some money for/putting some money by for

questions.sentence:
Did your mum gain any money when she sold her business?
**profit** Did your mum ...... when she sold her business?

Not many people want this type of bank account.
**demand** There is ...... this type of bank account.
questions.answer: little demand for/not much demand for

questions.sentence:
I didn't want to owe Sue money, so I didn't borrow any.
**debt** I didn't want ...... Sue, so I didn't borrow any money.
questions.answer: to be in debt to/to be into debt to/to get in debt to/to get into debt to

questions.sentence:
You must have paid a lot of money for such a nice engagement ring, Jim!
**fortune** A nice engagement ring like that must ...... Jim!

Inflation has gone up by 3% this year.
**increase** There has been an ...... 3% this year.

You won't have any money if you keep buying CDs.
**it** You won't have any money if you keep ...... CDs.

Don't spend money on hiring a limousine at the airport.
**expense** Don't go ...... hiring a limousine at the airport.

I had to pay £50 for breaking the vase in the shop!
**charged** They ...... £50 for breaking the vase in the shop!

A lot of money was stolen from the bank in the robbery.
**amount** The bank lost ...... in the robbery.

I can't afford a holiday this year.
**enough** I don't have ...... on holiday this year.

I succeeded through hard work, clever decisions and, finally but importantly, luck.
**least** I succeeded through hard work, clever decisions and, ......, luck.

The shoplifter took a packet of crisps without the shopkeeper seeing.
**notice** The shopkeeper didn't ...... a packet of crisps.
questions.answer: notice the shoplifter taking/notice the shoplifter take

questions.sentence:
I'll go and buy what we need and you start cooking.
**shopping** I'll go and ...... and you start cooking.
I can't believe they charged me **E** ...for using the hotel pool.
The old lady begged me **G** ...to give her some money.
I'll never forget **C** ...learning that I had the winning ticket.
My dad and my uncle always argue **A** ...about the economy.
I was shocked when the bank demanded **B** ...that I pay back my loan immediately.
We really can't afford **F** ...to buy a new fridge.
Debra paid the money **D** ...to the girl at the desk and left.

G.title: Word patterns (gap fill)
G.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

questions.sentence: Europe is heading for a financial crisis, or so some analysts believe. Although we are spending more money (1) ...... luxury items and recreation than ever before, it is often money we have borrowed (2) ...... a bank.
Banks are willing to lend more and more money (3) ...... us because they profit (4) ...... it by charging us interest.
We are spending money that doesn't really belong (5) ...... us, and few of us save much (6) ...... our old age.
